author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a well-known title; and it is not Christ who speaks; but the prophet speaks to Christ, under the form of wishing, foretelling things to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hear Thee in the day of trouble” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord hear Thee in the day in which Thou saidst, “Father glorify Thy Son.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The name of the God of Jacob protect Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to Thee belongeth the younger people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since “the elder shall serve the younger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Send Thee help from the Holy, and from Sion defend Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Making for Thee a sanctified Body, the Church, from watching safe, which waiteth when Thou shalt come from the wedding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be mindful of all Thy sacrifice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make us mindful of all Thy injuries and despiteful treatment, which Thou hast borne for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And be Thy whole burnt offering made fat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And turn the cross, whereon Thou wast wholly offered up to God, into the joy of the resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Diapsalma.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord render to Thee according to Thine Heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord render to Thee, not according to their heart, who thought by persecution they could destroy Thee; but according to Thine Heart, wherein Thou knewest what profit Thy passion would have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And fulfil all Thy counsel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And fulfil all Thy counsel,” not only that whereby Thou didst lay down Thy life for Thy friends, that the corrupted grain might rise again to more abundance; but that also whereby “blindness in part hath happened unto Israel, that the fulness of the Gentiles might enter in, and so all Israel might be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We will exult in Thy salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will exult in that death will in no wise hurt Thee; for so Thou wilt also show that it cannot hurt us either.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in the name of the Lord our God will we be magnified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the confession of Thy name shall not only not destroy us, but shall even magnify us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord fulfil all Thy petitions.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord fulfil not only the petitions which Thou madest on earth, but those also whereby Thou intercedest for us in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Now have I known that the Lord hath saved his Christ” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now hath it been shown to me in prophecy, that the Lord will raise up His Christ again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will hear Him from His holy heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will hear Him not from earth only, where He prayed to be glorified; but from heaven also, where interceding for us at the Right Hand of the Father, He hath from thence shed abroad the Holy Spirit on them that believe on Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In strength is the safety of His right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our strength is in the safety of His favour, when even out of tribulation He giveth help, that “when we are weak, then we may be strong.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For vain is” that “safety of man,” which comes not of His right hand but of His left: for thereby are they lifted up to great pride, whosoever in their sins have secured a temporal safety.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Some in chariots, and some in horses” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some are drawn away by the ever moving succession of temporal goods; and some are preferred to proud honours, and in them exult: “But we will exult in the name of the Lord our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we, fixing our hope on things eternal, and not seeking our own glory, will exult in the name of the Lord our God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have been bound, and fallen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore were they bound by the lust of temporal things, fearing to spare the Lord, lest they should lose their place by “the Romans:” and rushing violently on the stone of offence and rock of stumbling, they fell from the heavenly hope: to whom the blindness in part of Israel hath happened, being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and wishing to establish their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But we are risen, and stand upright.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we, that the Gentile people might enter in, out of the stones raised up as children to Abraham, who followed not after righteousness, have attained to it, and are risen; and not by our own strength, but being justified by faith, we stand upright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, save the King:” that He, who in His Passion hath shown us an example of conflict, should also offer up our sacrifices, the Priest raised from the dead, and established in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hear us in the day when we shall call on Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as He now offereth for us, “hear us in the day when we shall call on Thee.”
